public final TableField<ChatLinkRecord, Integer> ID =
    createField(DSL.name("ID"), SQLDataType.INTEGER.nullable(false).identity(true), this, "");
Explanation: This junction table (CHAT_LINK) has a synthetic auto-increment primary key 'ID' when the natural composite key (CHAT_ID, LINK_ID) would be more appropriate. Consider using a composite primary key on (CHAT_ID, LINK_ID) instead of the synthetic ID column.

    createField(DSL.name("OPEN_ISSUES_COUNT"), SQLDataType.INTEGER, this, "");
Explanation: The OPEN_ISSUES_COUNT column is marked as NULLABLE but likely represents a count that should never be NULL in practice (counts are typically 0 or more). Consider making it NOT NULL with a default value of 0, or ensure NULL has a clear semantic meaning distinct from 0.

Using `Tables.LINK.fields()` selects all columns from the LINK table, which may fetch unnecessary data and reduce query performance. Instead, explicitly select only the columns needed for the Link class (e.g., `Tables.LINK.ID, Tables.LINK.URL`).
